description: Benchmark for evaluating context compression. Two parallel feature tracks (API + reports) create mixed-relevance context in later tasks.

tasks.name: Database schema
tasks.prompt:
Create src/db.js with a SQLite database using sql.js.

Define three tables:
- users: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), email (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL), password_hash (TEXT NOT NULL), created_at (TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
- tasks: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), user_id (INTEGER REFERENCES users), title (TEXT NOT NULL), description (TEXT), status (TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(status IN ('pending','in_progress','done'))), created_at (TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
- reports: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT), user_id (INTEGER REFERENCES users), title (TEXT NOT NULL), content_html (TEXT), generated_at (TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

Export the database instance and a function initDb() that creates the tables.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Markdown-to-HTML converter
tasks.prompt:
Create src/utils/markdown.js with a function that converts basic Markdown to HTML. Export convertMarkdown(text) that handles: - Headers: # H1, ## H2, ### H3 (convert to

,

,

) - Bold: **text** to text - Italic: *text* to text - Unordered lists: lines starting with - to
  • ...
- Paragraphs: blank-line-separated text wrapped in

tags - Code blocks: ```...``` to 

...
Use only regex, no external dependencies. Run tests after.

tasks.name: User authentication
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/auth.js with user registration and login endpoints.

POST /api/auth/register: accepts {email, password}, hash password with bcryptjs, insert into users table, return {id, email} with status 201.
POST /api/auth/login: accepts {email, password}, verify credentials, return {token} (JWT signed with process.env.JWT_SECRET or 'dev-secret') with status 200.

Return appropriate error responses: 409 for duplicate email, 401 for invalid credentials.

Mount the router in src/index.js under /api/auth.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Task CRUD endpoints
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/tasks.js with CRUD endpoints for the tasks table.

GET /api/tasks?user_id=N: list all tasks for a user.
POST /api/tasks: create a task {user_id, title, description}, return the created task with status 201.
PUT /api/tasks/:id: update a task {title, description, status}, return updated task.
DELETE /api/tasks/:id: delete a task, return status 204.

For now, accept user_id as a query/body param. Auth middleware will be added later.

Mount the router in src/index.js under /api/tasks.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Weekly report generator
tasks.prompt:
Create src/reports.js with a report generation module.

Export generateWeeklyReport(userId) that:
1. Queries the tasks table for all tasks created in the last 7 days for the given user
2. Groups tasks by status (pending, in_progress, done)
3. Builds a Markdown string with sections for each status group, listing task titles
4. Converts the Markdown to HTML using the convertMarkdown utility from src/utils/markdown.js
5. Inserts a row into the reports table with the generated HTML
6. Returns {id, title, content_html, generated_at}

The report title should be 'Weekly Report - {date}'.

Also add a GET /api/reports?user_id=N endpoint in a new src/routes/reports.js that lists all reports for a user. Mount it in src/index.js.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Input validation middleware
tasks.prompt:
Create src/middleware/validate.js with request validation middleware.

Export functions:
- validateTask(req, res, next): validates POST/PUT /api/tasks body. Title is required and must be a non-empty string. Status if provided must be one of: pending, in_progress, done. Description is optional. Return 400 with {error: 'message'} on failure.
- validateAuth(req, res, next): validates POST /api/auth body. Email must contain @ and a dot. Password must be at least 8 characters. Return 400 with {error: 'message'} on failure.

Apply validateTask to the task routes and validateAuth to the auth routes by updating their respective route files.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Auth middleware for task routes
tasks.prompt:
Create src/middleware/auth.js with JWT authentication middleware.

Export authenticateToken(req, res, next):
- Read the Authorization header (Bearer <token>)
- Verify the JWT with process.env.JWT_SECRET or 'dev-secret'
- Set req.userId from the token payload
- Return 401 with {error: 'unauthorized'} if token is missing or invalid

Apply this middleware to all /api/tasks routes so they require authentication. Update the task routes to use req.userId instead of the user_id query/body param.

Run tests after.

tasks.name: Task CRUD integration tests
tasks.prompt:
Create src/routes/tasks.test.js with integration tests for the task CRUD endpoints using Node's built-in test runner (node:test).

Test:
- GET /api/tasks returns only tasks belonging to the authenticated user
- POST /api/tasks creates a task and returns 201
- POST /api/tasks without title returns 400 (validation)
- PUT /api/tasks/:id updates the task
- PUT /api/tasks/:id with invalid status returns 400
- DELETE /api/tasks/:id removes the task and returns 204
- All task endpoints return 401 without auth token

Setup: register a test user via POST /api/auth/register, login to get a token, use that token for all task requests. Use a test database (set NODE_ENV=test) to avoid data pollution.

Run the tests and ensure they pass.
